controls.control_objective: To ensure be called the reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio) directions, 2025
controls.control_description:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be called the reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio) directions, 2025

controls.control_objective: To ensure come into force with immediate effect
controls.control_description: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to come into force with immediate effect

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applicable to rural co-operative banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')
controls.control_description:
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applicable to rural co-operative banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean state co-operative banks (stcbs) and district central co-operative banks (dccbs) as defined in the national bank for agriculture and rural develo
controls.control_description:
In this context, 'Rural Co-operative Banks' shall mean State Co-operative Banks
(StCBs) and District Central Co-operative Banks (DCCBs) as defined in the National
Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean state co-operative banks (stcbs) and district central co-operative banks (dccbs) as defined in the national bank for agriculture and rural develo

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reported to the rbi under the following statutory returns: (1) form b return for scheduled co-operative banks
controls.control_description:
The maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) shall be reported to the RBI
under the following statutory returns:
(1) Form B Return for Scheduled Co-operative Banks.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reported to the rbi under the following statutory returns: (1) form b return for scheduled co-operative banks

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reported to rbi under the statutory return form i return (for slr) for all co-operative banks under section 24 of the banking regulation act, 1949
controls.control_description:
The maintenance of Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) shall be reported to RBI under
the statutory return Form I Return (for SLR) for all Co-operative Banks under
Section 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, read with Section 56 thereof.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reported to rbi under the statutory return form i return (for slr) for all co-operative banks under section 24 of the banking regulation act, 1949

controls.control_objective: To ensure bear the meaning assigned to them below: <image> (1) 'aggregate deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits
controls.control_description:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meaning assigned to them below:
<image>
(1) 'Aggregate Deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to bear the meaning assigned to them below: <image> (1) 'aggregate deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits

controls.control_objective: To ensure undertake the apportionment of saving bank account into demand liability and time liability as per the following procedure:
controls.control_description:
(2) 'Apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability and time liability': a
bank shall undertake the apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability
and time liability as per the following procedure:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to undertake the apportionment of saving bank account into demand liability and time liability as per the following procedure:

controls.control_objective: To ensure to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as at the close of business on march 31 and september 30, into demand and time liabilities in
controls.control_description:
(i) A bank is required to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as
at the close of business on March 31 and September 30, into demand and
time liabilities in terms of Regulation 7 of The Reserve Bank of India
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as at the close of business on march 31 and september 30, into demand and time liabilities in

controls.control_objective: To ensure be shown against liability to others
controls.control_description:
Explanation:
Explanation: Money at Call and Short Notice from outside the banking system shall
be shown against liability to others.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be shown against liability to others

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean the principal co-operative society in a district in a state, the primary object of which is financing of other co- operative societies in that di
controls.control_description:
(13) 'District Central Co-operative Bank' shall mean the Principal Co-operative
Society in a district in a State, the primary object of which is financing of other Co-
operative Societies in that district:
Provided that in addition to such Principal Society in a district, or where there is no
such Principal Society in a district, the State Government may declare any one or
more Co-operative Societies carrying on the business of financing other Co-
operative Societies in that district to be also or to be a District Central Co-operative
Bank or District Central Co-operative Banks within the meaning of this definition.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean the principal co-operative society in a district in a state, the primary object of which is financing of other co- operative societies in that di

controls.control_objective: To ensure the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days inclusive
controls.control_description:
(14) 2'Fortnight' means the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each
calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days
inclusive.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days inclusive

controls.control_objective: To ensure consist of investment in approved government securities and other approved securities (as explained below)
controls.control_description:
(15) 'Investment in India' shall consist of investment in approved government
securities and other approved securities (as explained below).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to consist of investment in approved government securities and other approved securities (as explained below)

controls.control_objective: To ensure include both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book
controls.control_description:
These shall include
both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean government securities, other than the securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above
controls.control_description:
(21) 'Other Approved Securities' shall mean Government Securities, other than the
securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above, subject to the condition that they are
notified as approved securities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean government securities, other than the securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above

controls.control_objective: To ensure include the following: explanation: definition
controls.control_description:
(22) 'Other Demand and Time Liabilities (ODTL)' shall include the following:
Explanation:
definition.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include the following: explanation: definition

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean deposits other than demand deposits
controls.control_description: (26) 'Time Deposits' shall mean deposits other than demand deposits.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean deposits other than demand deposits

controls.control_objective: To ensure include those liabilities which are payable otherwise than on demand and shall include the following: i
controls.control_description:
(27) 'Time Liabilities' of a bank shall include those liabilities which are payable
otherwise than on demand and shall include the following:
i.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include those liabilities which are payable otherwise than on demand and shall include the following: i

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the same meaning as have been assigned to them under the banking regulation act or the reserve bank of india act, or any statutory modification o
controls.control_description:
(28) All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act or the Reserve Bank
of India Act, or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the same meaning as have been assigned to them under the banking regulation act or the reserve bank of india act, or any statutory modification o

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated by the bank as the amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits
controls.control_description:
(ii) The average of the minimum balances maintained (in each account) in each
of the months during the half year period shall be treated by the bank as the
amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated by the bank as the amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year
controls.control_description:
(iii) The proportions of demand and time liabilities so obtained for each half year
shall be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of
savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year

controls.control_objective: To ensure be considered as approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as slr securities):
controls.control_description:
(3) 'Approved Securities/SLR securities': Following securities shall be considered as
approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as SLR securities):
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be considered as approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as slr securities):

controls.control_objective: To ensure be notified by the reserve bank of india (as and when prescribed)
controls.control_description:
(iv) Any other instrument as may be notified by the Reserve Bank of India (as and
when prescribed).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be notified by the reserve bank of india (as and when prescribed)

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain in india by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to such percent of the total of its ndtl in india, in such manner and for such dates, as th
controls.control_description:
Every bank shall maintain in India by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to
such percent of the total of its NDTL in India, in such manner and for such dates, as
the Reserve Bank in terms of Section 42(1) of the RBI Act, 1934 and Section 18(1)
of BR Act, 1949 [including provisions of Section 18 (1) of the BR Act as applicable to
cooperative banks], may specify, by notification in the Official Gazette, from time to
time having regard to the needs of securing the monetary stability in the country.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain in india by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to such percent of the total of its ndtl in india, in such manner and for such dates, as th

controls.control_objective: To ensure require the
controls.control_description: In terms of Section 42(1A) of RBI Act, 1934, the Reserve Bank may require the
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to require the

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than the rate specified by the reserve bank in the notification published in the gazette of india from time to time
controls.control_description:
scheduled banks to maintain, in addition to the balances prescribed under Section
42(1) of the Act, an additional average daily balance, the amount of which shall not
be less than the rate specified by the Reserve Bank in the notification published in
the Gazette of India from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than the rate specified by the reserve bank in the notification published in the gazette of india from time to time

controls.control_objective: To ensure be calculated with reference to the excess of the total of ndtl of the bank as shown in the returns referred to in section 42(2) of the rbi act, 1934
controls.control_description:
Provided that such additional balance shall be calculated with reference to the
excess of the total of NDTL of the bank as shown in the Returns referred to in
Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934 over the total of its NDTL at the close of the
business on the date specified in the notification.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be calculated with reference to the excess of the total of ndtl of the bank as shown in the returns referred to in section 42(2) of the rbi act, 1934

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than 3
controls.control_description:
Every scheduled bank shall maintain in India with the Reserve Bank, an average
daily balance, the amount of which shall not be less than 3.75 per cent, 3.5 per cent,
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than 3

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain in india on daily basis by way of cash reserve with itself
controls.control_description:
Every co-operative bank, (not being a scheduled co-operative bank), shall
maintain in India on daily basis by way of cash reserve with itself;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain in india on daily basis by way of cash reserve with itself

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than 3
controls.control_description:
or in one or more
of the aforesaid ways, the amount of which shall not be less than 3.75 per cent, 3.5
per cent, 3.25 per cent and 3.0 per cent of its NDTL, as on the last day of the second
<image>
preceding fortnight, effective from the reporting fortnight beginning September 6,
October 4, November 1 and November 29, 2025, respectively.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than 3

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than the crr prescribed by the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Every scheduled bank shall maintain minimum CRR of not less than 90 percent
of the required CRR on all days during the reporting fortnight, in such a manner that
the average of CRR maintained daily shall not be less than the CRR prescribed by
the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than the crr prescribed by the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be regarded as liability in india of a bank
controls.control_description:
For the purpose of these Directions, the Reserve Bank may specify from time to
time with reference to any transaction or class of transactions that such transaction
or transactions shall be regarded as liability in India of a bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be regarded as liability in india of a bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be regarded, for the purpose of these directions, as liability in india of a bank, the bank shall approach the rbi
controls.control_description:
If any question arises as to whether any transaction or transactions shall be
regarded, for the purpose of these Directions, as liability in India of a bank, the bank
shall approach the RBI.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be regarded, for the purpose of these directions, as liability in india of a bank, the bank shall approach the rbi

controls.control_objective: To ensure be final
controls.control_description: The decision of the Reserve Bank thereon shall be final.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be final

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as 'liabilities to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements
controls.control_description:
Loans / borrowings from abroad by banks in India shall be reckoned as
'liabilities to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as 'liabilities to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as liability for the computation of ndtl for the purpose of reserve requirements
controls.control_description:
Upper Tier II instruments raised and maintained in India / abroad shall be
reckoned as liability for the computation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve
requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as liability for the computation of ndtl for the purpose of reserve requirements

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as 'liability to others in india' for the computation of ndtl
controls.control_description:
The balance amount in respect of the drafts issued by the accepting bank on its
correspondent bank under the Remittance Facilities Scheme and remaining unpaid
shall be reckoned as 'Liability to others in India' for the computation of NDTL.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as 'liability to others in india' for the computation of ndtl

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as 'liability to the <image> banking system' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the inter-bank assets
controls.control_description:
The
amount received by correspondent banks shall be reckoned as 'Liability to the
<image>
Banking System' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks
against the inter-bank assets.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as 'liability to the <image> banking system' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the inter-bank assets

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as 'assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them off from their inter-bank liabilities
controls.control_description:
Sums placed by banks for issuing drafts / interest / dividend warrants shall be
treated as 'Assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them
off from their inter-bank liabilities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as 'assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them off from their inter-bank liabilities

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis
controls.control_description:
scheduled banks in respect of their savings bank deposits on the basis of the
position as at the close of business on 30th September and 31st March every year
shall continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product
basis.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis

controls.control_objective: To ensure form part of liabilities of a bank for the purpose of crr and slr: (1) paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for tier1
controls.control_description:
The liabilities mentioned below shall not form part of liabilities of a bank for the
purpose of CRR and SLR:
(1) Paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for Tier1
and additional Tier1 capital;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to form part of liabilities of a bank for the purpose of crr and slr: (1) paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for tier1

controls.control_objective: To ensure have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve requirements
controls.control_description:
Provided that the funds collected by various branches of the bank or other banks for
the issue and held pending finalization of allotment of the additional Tier1 preference
shares shall have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve
requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve requirements

controls.control_objective: To ensure be included for ndtl computation for slr purposes (in <image> case of scheduled stcbs) and for both crr and slr purposes (in case of other stcbs / dis
controls.control_description:
Provided that any advance or other credit arrangement drawn and availed against
approved securities shall not be included for NDTL computation for SLR purposes (in
<image>
case of Scheduled StCBs) and for both CRR and SLR purposes (in case of other
StCBs / District Central Co-operative Banks).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be included for ndtl computation for slr purposes (in <image> case of scheduled stcbs) and for both crr and slr purposes (in case of other stcbs / dis

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and institutions to the state co-operative bank
controls.control_description:
(d) any other financial institution notified by the Central Government in this
behalf,
shall be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and
institutions to the State co-operative bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and institutions to the state co-operative bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be included as part of bank credit for the purpose of these directions
controls.control_description:
Loans out of Foreign Currency Non-Resident Accounts (Banks), (FCNR [B]
Deposits Scheme) and Inter-Bank Foreign Currency (IBFC) deposits shall be
included as part of bank credit for the purpose of these Directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be included as part of bank credit for the purpose of these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure use 4 amended with effect from june 8, 2026 vide reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio) s
controls.control_description:
Banks shall use
4 Amended with effect from June 8, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Second Amendment Directions, 2026 dated June 8, 2026 5 Amended with effect from June 19, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Third Amendment Directions, 2026 dated June 19, 2026
<image>
the conversion rate announced by Financial Benchmarks India Private Limited (FBIL)
for the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for reporting in the statutory
returns mentioned at paragraphs 4 and 5 of these directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to use 4 amended with effect from june 8, 2026 vide reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio) s

controls.control_objective: To ensure use new york closing rate pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into usd and then use the reference rate of fb
controls.control_description:
For conversion of
assets / liabilities in other currencies, banks may use New York Closing Rate
pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into
USD and then use the reference rate of FBIL for USD/ INR for the same day for
conversion into INR.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to use new york closing rate pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into usd and then use the reference rate of fb

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second preced
controls.control_description:
Every bank, in addition to the cash reserves which it is required to maintain
under these Directions, shall maintain in India, assets, the value of which shall not
be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand
and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second preceding fortnight as
the Reserve Bank may, by notification in the Official Gazette, specify from time to
time and such assets shall be maintained in such form and such manner, as may be
specified in such notification.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second preced

controls.control_objective: To ensure , at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 percent of its total net demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second
controls.control_description:
Every state co-operative bank and district central co-operative bank shall
maintain in India assets (hereinafter referred to as 'SLR assets') the value of which
shall not, at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 percent of its total net
demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second preceding
fortnight in accordance with the method of valuation specified by RBI from time to
time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 percent of its total net demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the option to participate in the marginal standing facility (msf) scheme introduced by the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Banks permitted by Reserve Bank shall have the option to participate in the
Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the option to participate in the marginal standing facility (msf) scheme introduced by the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the option to borrow up to two per cent of their respective ndtl outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight
controls.control_description:
The
features of the scheme are:
(1) The eligible banks shall have the option to borrow up to two per cent of their
respective NDTL outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the option to borrow up to two per cent of their respective ndtl outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight

controls.control_objective: To ensure also continue to access overnight funds under this facility against their excess slr holdings
controls.control_description:
(2) The eligible entities shall also continue to access overnight funds under this
facility against their excess SLR holdings.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also continue to access overnight funds under this facility against their excess slr holdings

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the obligation to seek a specific waiver for default in slr compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification issued under
controls.control_description:
(3) In the event of banks' SLR holding falling below the statutory requirement up to
two percent of their NDTL, banks shall not have the obligation to seek a specific
waiver for default in SLR compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of
notification issued under sub section (2A) of Section 24 of the Banking Regulation
Act, 1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the obligation to seek a specific waiver for default in slr compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification issued under

controls.control_objective: To ensure be maintained by stcbs and dccbs as under: <image> (1) cash, or
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be maintained by stcbs and dccbs as under: <image> (1) cash, or

controls.control_objective: To ensure be considered as eligible assets for slr maintenance
controls.control_description:
(3) Unencumbered investment in approved securities as defined in section 5(a) of
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (10 of 1949) read with section 56 thereof:
Provided that the instruments that have been acquired from Reserve Bank of India
under reverse repo, shall be considered as eligible assets for SLR maintenance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be considered as eligible assets for slr maintenance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as encumbered for the purpose of maintenance of slr assets, namely
controls.control_description:
Provided further that the following securities shall not be treated as encumbered
for the purpose of maintenance of SLR assets, namely
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as encumbered for the purpose of maintenance of slr assets, namely

controls.control_objective: To ensure be exempted from crr/slr computation and the security acquired under repo shall be eligible for slr provided the security is primarily eligible for sl
controls.control_description:
(ii) Funds borrowed under repo including tri-party repo in government securities
shall be exempted from CRR/SLR computation and the security acquired
under repo shall be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible
for SLR as per the provisions of the Act under which it is required to be
maintained.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be exempted from crr/slr computation and the security acquired under repo shall be eligible for slr provided the security is primarily eligible for sl

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as liabilities for cash reserve ratio/ statutory liquidity ratio requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking syste
controls.control_description:
(iii) Borrowings by a bank through repo in corporate bonds and debentures shall
be reckoned as liabilities for Cash Reserve Ratio/ Statutory Liquidity Ratio
requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking system, they
shall be netted as per Section 42 (1) (d) of the RBI Act, 1934.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as liabilities for cash reserve ratio/ statutory liquidity ratio requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking syste

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain investments in government securities only in subsidiary general ledger (sgl) accounts with reserve bank or in csgl <image> accounts of schedu
controls.control_description:
(iv) All banks shall maintain investments in Government Securities only in
Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts with Reserve Bank or in CSGL
<image>
Accounts of scheduled commercial banks, Primary Dealers (PDs), State Co-
operative Banks, and Stock Holding Corporation of India Ltd.(SHCIL) or in the
dematerialised accounts with depositories such as National Securities
Depositories Ltd (NSDL), Central Depository Services Ltd.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain investments in government securities only in subsidiary general ledger (sgl) accounts with reserve bank or in csgl <image> accounts of schedu

controls.control_objective: To ensure report the sdf balances held by banks with rbi in form i, as it is an eligible asset for slr maintenance
controls.control_description:
(v) 6Banks shall report the SDF balances held by banks with RBI in Form I, as it
is an eligible asset for SLR maintenance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to report the sdf balances held by banks with rbi in form i, as it is an eligible asset for slr maintenance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be eligible for cash reserve ratio (crr) maintenance
controls.control_description:
The balances held by banks with
RBI under the SDF shall not be eligible for Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR)
maintenance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be eligible for cash reserve ratio (crr) maintenance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as government of india treasury bill and thus be reckoned as slr security
controls.control_description:
(ii) The cash management bill shall be treated as Government of India Treasury
Bill and thus be reckoned as SLR security.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as government of india treasury bill and thus be reckoned as slr security

controls.control_objective: To ensure be as below: (1) total ndtl for the purpose of slr under section 24 (2a) of banking regulation act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure a
controls.control_description:
The procedure for computation of NDTL for SLR shall be as below:
(1) Total NDTL for the purpose of SLR under Section 24 (2A) of Banking
Regulation Act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure as followed for
CRR.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be as below: (1) total ndtl for the purpose of slr under section 24 (2a) of banking regulation act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure a

controls.control_objective: To ensure form
controls.control_description: (2) The liabilities mentioned under paragraph 20 of these Directions shall not form
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to form

controls.control_objective: To ensure include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of all maturities in 'assets with the banking system' for computation of ndtl for sl
controls.control_description:
(3) Banks shall include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of
all maturities in 'Assets with the Banking System' for computation of NDTL for SLR
purpose.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of all maturities in 'assets with the banking system' for computation of ndtl for sl

controls.control_objective: To ensure be in accordance with the extant instructions reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - classification, valuation and operation of investment
controls.control_description:
Classification and valuation of approved securities shall be in accordance with
the extant instructions Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions,
2025, as applicable.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be in accordance with the extant instructions reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - classification, valuation and operation of investment

controls.control_objective: To ensure submit to reserve bank a return in form 'b' (annex i) at the close of business on 9last day of each fortnight within five days after the date of the r
controls.control_description:
Under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, every Scheduled State Co-operative
bank shall submit to Reserve Bank a Return in Form 'B' (Annex I) at the close of
business on 9last day of each fortnight within five days after the date of the relevant
fortnight to which it relates..
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to submit to reserve bank a return in form 'b' (annex i) at the close of business on 9last day of each fortnight within five days after the date of the r

controls.control_objective: To ensure give at the close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate t
controls.control_description:
Where such last day of each fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act, 1881, for one or more offices of the bank, the Return shall give at
the close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office
or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of such fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give at the close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate t

controls.control_objective: To ensure be submitted to reserve bank along with: (1) the memorandum to the return in form 'b' giving details of paid-up capital, reserves, time deposits compr
controls.control_description:
The Return in Form 'B' shall be submitted to Reserve Bank along with:
(1) the Memorandum to the Return in Form 'B' giving details of paid-up capital,
reserves, time deposits comprising short-term (of contractual maturity of one year or
less) and long-term (of contractual maturity of more than one year), certificates of
deposits, NDTL, total CRR requirement, etc.,
(2) Annex I to the Return in Form 'B' showing all foreign currency liabilities and
assets, and
(3) Annex II to return in Form 'B' giving details about investment in approved
securities, investment in unapproved securities, memo items such as subscription to
shares / debentures / bonds in primary market and subscriptions through private
placement.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be submitted to reserve bank along with: (1) the memorandum to the return in form 'b' giving details of paid-up capital, reserves, time deposits compr

controls.control_objective: To ensure submit a return in form i (annex ii) together with appendix i, ii and iii, to the regional 9 amended with effect from december 15, 2025 vide reserve b
controls.control_description:
Every co-operative bank, not being a scheduled co-operative bank, shall submit
a Return in Form I (Annex II) together with Appendix I, II and III, to the regional
9 Amended with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025 10 Deleted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025
<image>
office concerned of the Reserve Bank, not later than 20 days after the end of the
month to which it relates showing the position, inter alia, of cash reserves maintained
by the bank under Section 18 of the B.R.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to submit a return in form i (annex ii) together with appendix i, ii and iii, to the regional 9 amended with effect from december 15, 2025 vide reserve b

controls.control_objective: To ensure give the preceding day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight
controls.control_description:
Where such day
is a public holiday under Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 for one or more offices of
the bank, the Return shall give the preceding day's figure in respect of such office or
offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give the preceding day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight

controls.control_objective: To ensure give reasons therefor in the return
controls.control_description:
Whenever there are wide variations between the sources and uses of funds as
being reported in the fortnightly Return and the variations exceed 20 per cent, the
banks concerned should give reasons therefor in the Return.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give reasons therefor in the return

controls.control_objective: To ensure to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorized to sign on behalf o
controls.control_description:
In terms of Regulation 5(i) (c) of the Scheduled Banks Regulations,1951 and
Regulation 4(1) of the Banking Regulation (Co-operative Societies) Rules, 1966, the
banks are required to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and
specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorized to sign on behalf
of the banks, Returns prescribed under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, and
Section 18 and 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorized to sign on behalf o

controls.control_objective: To ensure be no provisional or final or special form b return
controls.control_description:
11Under the new reporting structure, as amended vide Banking Laws
(Amendment) Act 2025, there shall be no Provisional or Final or Special Form B
return.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be no provisional or final or special form b return

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit single form b return
controls.control_description: The banks are required to submit single Form B return.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit single form b return

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit fortnightly form b return from december 15, 2025 and monthly form i return from december 2025 on the cims portal
controls.control_description:
The banks are required to submit
fortnightly Form B return from December 15, 2025 and monthly Form I return from
December 2025 on the CIMS portal.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit fortnightly form b return from december 15, 2025 and monthly form i return from december 2025 on the cims portal

controls.control_objective: To ensure be done, as 11 inserted with effect from december 15, 2025 vide reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liq
controls.control_description:
From the subsequent
fortnights starting from January 16, 2026, the maintenance shall be done, as
11 Inserted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025 12 Inserted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025
<image>
presently applicable, i.e., based on the NDTL as on the last day of the second
preceding fortnight
37 C.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be done, as 11 inserted with effect from december 15, 2025 vide reserve bank of india (rural co-operative banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liq

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain minimum crr of not less than hundred per cent of the required crr during the transition period
controls.control_description:
Further, the banks
shall maintain minimum CRR of not less than hundred per cent of the required CRR
during the transition period.The banks shall also submit Form B return for December
12, 2025, as per the extant reporting structure.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain minimum crr of not less than hundred per cent of the required crr during the transition period

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit a return in form i (annex ii) under section 24 of the br act, 1949 (aacs) every month showing the position of liquid assets maintained under
controls.control_description:
All Co-operative Banks (scheduled and non-scheduled), are required to submit
a Return in Form I (Annex II) under Section 24 of the BR Act, 1949 (AACS) every
month showing the position of liquid assets maintained under the said Section as at
the close of business on the last day of each fortnight during the month not later than
twenty days after the end of the month to which it relates.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit a return in form i (annex ii) under section 24 of the br act, 1949 (aacs) every month showing the position of liquid assets maintained under

controls.control_objective: To ensure verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities, as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under ndt
controls.control_description:
The Statutory Auditors shall verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities,
as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected
under NDTL in the fortnightly / monthly statutory returns submitted to Reserve Bank
for the financial year.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities, as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under ndt

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain a register, as per format given in annex iii showing the daily position of cash reserve and liquid assets maintained under sections 18 and 24
controls.control_description:
All co-operative banks shall maintain a register, as per format given in Annex
III showing the daily position of cash reserve and liquid assets maintained under
Sections 18 and 24 of the Banking regulation Act, 1949 read with Section 56 thereof
which shall be put up daily to Chief Executive Officer, who is responsible for ensuring
13 Inserted with effect from December 12, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025
<image>
compliance with the statutory liquidity requirements at the close of business every
day.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain a register, as per format given in annex iii showing the daily position of cash reserve and liquid assets maintained under sections 18 and 24

controls.control_objective: To ensure be recovered from all scheduled state co-operative banks in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed crr on a daily basis for that day at t
controls.control_description:
(1) Penal interest shall be recovered from all Scheduled State Co-operative Banks in
the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed CRR on a daily basis for that day
at the rate of three percent per annum above the Bank Rate on the amount by which
the amount actually maintained falls short of the prescribed minimum on that day
and if the shortfall continues on the next succeeding day/s, penal interest shall be
recovered at the rate of five percent per annum above the Bank Rate.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be recovered from all scheduled state co-operative banks in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed crr on a daily basis for that day at t

controls.control_objective: To ensure be liable to pay to the reserve bank, penal interest as envisaged in sub- section (1-a) of section 18 read with section 56 of the b
controls.control_description:
(3) In the case of a co-operative bank, not being a scheduled co-operative bank, the
bank shall be liable to pay to the Reserve Bank, penal interest as envisaged in sub-
section (1-A) of Section 18 read with Section 56 of the B.R.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be liable to pay to the reserve bank, penal interest as envisaged in sub- section (1-a) of section 18 read with section 56 of the b

controls.control_objective: To ensure to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage, reason for default in maintenance of requisite crr and also action taken to avoid recurre
controls.control_description:
Banks are required to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage,
reason for default in maintenance of requisite CRR and also action taken to avoid
recurrence of such default.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage, reason for default in maintenance of requisite crr and also action taken to avoid recurre

controls.control_objective: To ensure be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during which default c
controls.control_description:
Under the provisions of Section 42(3A) of the RBI Act, 1934, penal interest at
the increased rate of five per cent above the Bank Rate become payable and if the
default still continues during the next succeeding fortnight:
(1) Every Director, Manager or Secretary of the scheduled bank who is knowingly
and willfully a party to the default, shall be punishable with fine which may extend to
₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight
during which default continues.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during which default c

controls.control_objective: To ensure in respect of each such default be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each day after t
controls.control_description:
(2) The Reserve Bank may prohibit a scheduled bank from receiving any fresh
deposit after the said fortnight, and if default is made by the bank in complying with
<image>
the prohibition referred to in this clause, every director and officer of the bank who is
knowingly and willfully a party to such default or who through negligence or otherwise
contributes to such default shall in respect of each such default be punishable with
fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for
each day after the first, on which a deposit received in contravention of such
prohibition is retained by the scheduled bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to in respect of each such default be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each day after t

controls.control_objective: To ensure attract the provisions of section 42(4) of rbi act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of penalties as indicated therein
controls.control_description:
Failure to submit the Return/late submission of the Return shall attract the
provisions of Section 42(4) of RBI Act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of
penalties as indicated therein.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to attract the provisions of section 42(4) of rbi act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of penalties as indicated therein

controls.control_objective: To ensure be liable to pay to the reserve bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under section 24 read with section 56 of the br act
controls.control_description:
On the failure of the bank to maintain as on any day, the amount of SLR
required to be maintained by a bank, the bank shall be liable to pay to the Reserve
Bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under Section 24
read with Section 56 of the BR Act, 1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be liable to pay to the reserve bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under section 24 read with section 56 of the br act

controls.control_objective: To ensure be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of licensed banks and refuse licence in case of unlicensed banks under section 22 of the act
controls.control_description:
Where it is observed that banks are persistently defaulting despite instructions
and repeated advice, the Reserve Bank in addition to levy of penalty on such
defaulting banks, may be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of
licensed banks and refuse licence in case of unlicensed banks under Section 22 of
the Act, ibid.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of licensed banks and refuse licence in case of unlicensed banks under section 22 of the act

controls.control_objective: To ensure , therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of required re
controls.control_description:
The banks should, therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance
of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of
required Return to Regional Office concerned of Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of required re

controls.control_objective: To ensure be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity
controls.control_description:
Disclaimer: It is hereby advised that mere inclusion of any item in the above
Master Direction should not be construed as a permission to undertake all such
activities by a banking entity.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to remain repealed
controls.control_description:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issue of these Directions shall
continue to remain repealed.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to remain repealed

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to be governed by the provisions thereof
controls.control_description:
Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to be governed by the provisions thereof

controls.control_objective: To ensure be deemed as governed by these directions
controls.control_description:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be deemed as governed by these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure in any way prejudicially affect:
controls.control_description:
Further, the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way
prejudicially affect:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to in any way prejudicially affect:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline
controls.control_description:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

controls.control_objective: To ensure be in addition to, and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force
controls.control_description:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be in addition to, and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

controls.control_objective: To ensure be final and binding
controls.control_description:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be final and binding

controls.control_objective: To ensure give the preceding working day's figures in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnigh
controls.control_description: @ Where last day of the fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 (26 of 1881) for one or more offices of a scheduled State Co-operative bank, the Return shall give the preceding working day's figures in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give the preceding working day's figures in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnigh

controls.control_objective: To ensure the banks and any other financial institutions referred to in regulations (i) to (v) of clause (e) of the explanation below section 42(1) of the reser
controls.control_description: * The expression "Banking System" or "Banks" wherever it appears in the return means the banks and any other financial institutions referred to in regulations (i) to (v) of clause (e) of the Explanation below Section 42(1) of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the banks and any other financial institutions referred to in regulations (i) to (v) of clause (e) of the explanation below section 42(1) of the reser

controls.control_description:

included in the figure against II (c).

controls.control_description:

of 1881), the figures as at the close of business on the preceding working day should
be furnished.

controls.control_objective: To ensure include borrowings from a state government, the reserve bank, export-import bank of india, the national housing bank, the small industries bank, natio
controls.control_description:
For the purpose of this return, "liabilities in India to others" shall not include borrowings
from a State Government, the Reserve Bank, Export-Import Bank of India, the National
Housing Bank, the Small Industries Bank, National Bank for Agriculture and Rural
Development, or the National Co-operative Development Corporation established
under Section 3 of the National Co- operative Development Act, 1962
% (i) Any balance held by Co-operative Bank with another bank shall not, to the
extent such balance represents the investment of Agricultural Credit Stabilisation Fund
of such Co-operative Bank, be deemed to be cash maintained in India.

controls.control_objective: To ensure be deemed to be such cash maintained in india
controls.control_description:
(ii) In case the co-operative bank has taken an advance against any balance
maintained with the State Co-operative bank of the State concerned or with the
District Central Co-operative Bank of the district concerned, such balance to the
extent to which it has been drawn against or availed of shall not be deemed to
be such cash maintained in India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be deemed to be such cash maintained in india

controls.control_description:
& (i) For the purpose of this return, any cash with a Co-operative Bank shall not, to
the extent such cash represents the balance in Agricultural Credit Stabilisation
Fund of such Co-operative Bank, be deemed to be cash maintained in India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , to the extent such cash represents the balance in agricultural credit stabilisation fund of such co-operative bank, be deemed to be cash maintained
